steps.title: Identify the shared problem
steps.content:
Both authors diagnose the same issue: modernist scholarship's focus is too narrow, creating a limited picture of the period.
Text 1 shows overrepresentation of fragmented narratives like To the Lighthouse and underrepresentation of coherent ones like The Enchanted April.
Text 2 shows that focusing narrowly on “fragmentation versus coherence” inhibits inquiry into other stylistic elements.
The key signal: both criticize the limited scope of current scholarship.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
A directly states their shared agreement: without broadening focus, the scholarly vision will remain unnecessarily limited.
Text 1 proves this—von Armin stays overlooked when scholars spotlight fragmented narratives.
Text 2 makes the same point—the narrow focus “inhibits inquiry” into other stylistic elements.
The others fail because
  • B frames it as unproductive debate (the authors don't)
  • C claims methods become obsolete (they don't)
  • D moves beyond style when both texts focus on style
.
That matches choice A—A is correct.
